items.title: The National | Trump lashes out at allies over Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: March 17, 2026 | U.S. President Donald Trump lashes out at allies over their refusal to help in the Strait of Hormuz. Ontario's Provincial Police find no wrongdoing by Toronto officers accused of lying at a high-profile murder trial. And, the CFIA fines grocery stores for mislabelling their products as Canadian.

items.title: Netanyahu posts video to dispel rumours of his death after disinformation spreads online
items.summary_auto: ​Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu posted a video of himself getting a coffee on Sunday in an apparent attempt to prove that online rumours of his death have been greatly exaggerated.

items.title: Cuba reconnects electrical grid, but millions are still without power
items.summary_auto: Cuba has reconnected its electrical grid across much of the country, the Energy and Mines Ministry said early on Tuesday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump escalated his rhetoric against the communist-run ‌island, saying he could do anything he wanted with Cuba.

items.title: Longest ballot organizers eye protest in upcoming Montreal-area byelection
items.summary_auto: A group that has signed up droves of protest candidates in a number of elections and byelections in recent years is organizing in another upcoming vote, this time in Terrebonne.

items.title: Is Iran waging war with mines in the Strait of Hormuz? | About That
items.summary_auto: Andrew Chang explains what we know about Iran's sea mine capabilities and why they can be such a destructive and difficult naval threat to remove — especially in a critical shipping route. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters, Adobe Stock, CAT-UXO and Getty Images

items.title: 4-day work weeks, rationing, dressing down: How some Asian countries are coping with the Iran oil crisis
items.summary_auto: Much of the oil produced in the Middle East is exported to Asian countries, which are now using a range of different methods to adjust to the fuel crunch caused by Iran's partial blockage of the Strait of Hormuz amid its war with the U.S. and Israel.

items.title: Doug Armstrong steps down as general manager of Canada's Olympic hockey team
items.summary_auto: Doug Armstrong is stepping down from his position as general manager of Canada's Olympic hockey team. Hockey Canada says Armstrong informed the organization he will be focusing on his position with the St. Louis Blues going forward.

items.title: Police anticipated 'worst-case scenario' by deploying sniper at St. Patrick's Day party, says former officer
items.summary_auto: The sighting of a sniper on Saturday during St. Patrick’s Day celebrations in Waterloo was met with fear and shock by some, but one policing expert from Wilfrid Laurier University says this is a proactive measure designed to keep the crowd safe.

items.title: Federal government appeals Emergencies Act use during convoy protest to Supreme Court
items.summary_auto: The federal government is bringing its case to justify use of the Emergencies Act to clear the convoy protests that gridlocked the capital city and border points to the country's highest court.

items.title: Should we worry about Canada's winter sports decline?
items.summary_auto: Canada's Winter Olympic and Paralympic medal counts are falling. Is this the sign of a crisis, or merely a shift?

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Celebrating St. Patrick's Day in Canada and abroad
items.summary_auto: St. Patrick's Day is marked annually on March 17, the traditional death date of Ireland's patron saint, and has become a celebration of Irish culture and heritage worldwide.

items.title: The world's oldest-known whale song recordings tell a story about the changing ocean
items.summary_auto: Researchers at Woods Hole Oceanographic Institution have discovered a recording of humpback whales off the coast of Bermuda in March 1949 buried in their archives, marking the oldest-known recording of whale song.

items.title: The Strait of Hormuz kill box | About That
items.summary_auto: Andrew Chang explains why unblocking the world's most vital energy chokepoint is much harder than it may seem. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: 23 passengers booted from Saturday night Halifax-to-Cancun flight
items.summary_auto: Julia Kaiser, a spokesperson for WestJet, says an unexpected cabin crew absence meant that according to federal regulations, the number of passengers had to be cut to 150. There were 173 passengers ready to board.

items.title: Swapping stones led to Canada's 5th straight win at women's curling worlds
items.summary_auto: Canada's fifth straight win at the women's world curling championship was a lesson in adaptation for skip Kerri Einarson.

items.title: Former Bloc Québécois MP charged with sex crimes involving minors
items.summary_auto: The alleged incidents date back to 2006  — before Pascal-Pierre Paillé's election to the House of Commons — and as recently as August 2024.

items.title: Doctor Who fans get to step back in time with the discovery of 2 long-lost episodes
items.summary_auto: For decades, dozens of early Doctor Who episodes were feared to be lost forever — until two of them turned up recently in the garage of a deceased collector.

items.title: Oilers star Leon Draisaitl will miss rest of the regular season due to injury
items.summary_auto: Standout Edmonton Oilers forward Leon Draisaitl is expected to miss the rest of the NHL regular season because of what the team called a lower-body injury.

items.title: As Alberta’s population booms, report suggests Medicine Hat could fall behind
items.summary_auto: The city of Medicine Hat is one of few Alberta cities forecast for stagnant growth over the next 25 years as the rest of the province is expected to swell by two million more residents. Local officials are looking to draw new businesses and residents but experts say that will be a challenge.

items.title: International Olympic Committee urged to drop reported gender test plans for female athletes
items.summary_auto: More than 80 human rights and sport advocacy groups have called on the ‌International Olympic Committee to abandon reported plans to introduce universal genetic sex testing for female athletes and impose a blanket ban on transgender and intersex competitors.

items.title: Has Banksy’s true identity finally been revealed?
items.summary_auto: After decades of mystery, an investigation by Reuters, spanning from Ukraine to New York, may have just revealed the street artist Banksy’s identity. Reporter Blake Morrision, talks about his investigation and if knowing who the artist is changes our relationship with his work.

items.title: Fisheries officials investigating video of sailors bragging about giant crab catch near Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Parts of Vancouver's harbour are off-limits to crabbing, and while the men say they have stopped in "Vancouver, Canada," it's not clear exactly where the vessel is anchored.

items.title: Electricity demand, natural gas production and renewable power expected to soar by 2050
items.summary_auto: Electricity demand is set to boom in Canada by 2050, according to new modelling from the national energy regulator released on Tuesday.

items.title: Bilijk First Nation elects new chief Justice Gruben, youngest in N.B. at 28
items.summary_auto: Justice Gruben is the new chief of Bilijk, also known as Kingsclear First Nation. He wants to make change in the community for the next generation, which includes his two-year-old daughter.

items.title: 'Prolonged atmospheric river' brings heavy rain to B.C. coast
items.summary_auto: Rainfall warnings have been announced for parts of B.C. 's coast, with the heaviest rain expected on Vancouver Island and the Central Coast. Environment Canada says localized flooding is possible.

items.title: Queen of the chair: The legend of an Edmonton hairstyling icon known as Auntie Shirley
items.summary_auto: Shirley Romany is a legend in Edmonton's Black community and beyond. Nearly 60 years ago, she started Ebony and Ivory Salon, a gathering place where people of all backgrounds and ages feel seen, supported and at home.

items.title: Top U.S. counterterrorism official Joe Kent resigns over Trump's war on Iran
items.summary_auto: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, announced his resignation on Tuesday, saying he "cannot in good conscience" back the Trump administration's war in Iran.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Daily life amid Cuba's nationwide blackout
items.summary_auto: Cuba faces a nationwide blackout as its energy and economic crises deepen and its power grid continues to crumble.

items.title: Thinking of moving to a more 'affordable' part of the country? Consider this
Solo drivers will soon be able to use HOV lanes in off-peak hours: transportation minister
items.summary_auto: Highway lanes designated for carpoolers will soon be opened up to solo drivers at certain times of the day, Ontario's transportation minister said Tuesday.

items.title: Parental mistrust, hostile interactions a growing concern for Alberta teachers
items.summary_auto: When CBC News asked teachers what we should pay attention to in education, some flagged the growing mistrust that’s hurting relationships with parents and in some cases growing volatile. Here’s what we learned.

items.title: U.S. government confirms Tesla and LG Energy Solution large battery deal
items.summary_auto: LG also owns and operates NextStar, a similar battery cell factory in Windsor, Ont.

items.title: Holt government projects $6B added to debt, continued deficits through mandate
items.summary_auto: The finance minister had warned of a budget driven by difficult choices, but says talks about those options are still underway.

items.title: Canada's benchmark home price fell 4.8% in February compared to last year, says CREA
items.summary_auto: National homes sales edged down in February from the previous month, and the benchmark price of a home fell by 4.8 per cent compared to the same time last year, according to data from the Canadian Real Estate Association.

items.title: Iran confirms deaths of 2 senior security officials as war shows no signs of abating
items.summary_auto: Israel said Tuesday it killed two senior Iranian security officials in a major blow aimed at further weakening the Islamic Republic's leadership as it faces its greatest test in decades. Iran, which confirmed one killing, fired salvos of missiles and drones at its Gulf Arab neighbours and Israel.

items.title: N.L. restaurants being squeezed, say loan guarantee program no use
items.summary_auto: Last spring, the Newfoundland and Labrador government announced a loan guarantee program for restaurants — but so far, no businesses have tapped into it.

items.title: Her body was left on a couch for 6 months. Family, advocates say the system failed her
items.summary_auto: Two brothers avoided jail time after they admitted to keeping the body of Alasie Tukkiapik on their couch for six months in 2023. As the court case comes to a close, advocates and family members say the authorities failed the 41-year-old Inuk woman.

items.title: Another Loblaw store fined $10K for promoting imported food as Canadian. Sobeys could be next
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) is cracking down on grocery stores that promote imported food as Canadian. So far this year, the federal food regulator has issued two fines to Loblaw-owned grocery stores for this type of violation. The CFIA has also launched an investigation into labelling and advertising practices at grocer Sobeys’ head office.

items.title: Why allies aren't leaping to Trump's aid in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is struggling to persuade other countries to help protect commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz — a development analysts say is partly the result of how he has treated allies since returning to the White House last year.

items.title: Scott Pilgrim returns to brawl on the digital streets of Toronto in new video game
items.summary_auto: Toronto cartoonist Bryan Lee O'Malley continues to mark his comic book character Scott Pilgrim's 20th anniversary with the release of a new retro-styled video game.
